'''Viera Cheran''' served as [[Chief Technician]] for [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] and crew chief, responsible for overseeing the maintenance of the squadron's [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] and all flight deck operations. Known to many as simply "Chief", Cheran believed her job to be a thankless one. The fighter pilots got all the glory, while she and her crew got none.

==Biography==
[[File:Viera_Cheran_and_Wedge.jpg|thumb|left|Chief Cheran (right) reports to Wedge Antilles]]
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], [[Dark Prince|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]] of [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] intended to discredit [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] before [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] eyes. For that end he used his underground connections to bribe [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] and anyone else who could track down and kill [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]].

Shortly before the [[Battle of Gall]], a weary Cheran was offered 10,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] by an operative who wanted [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] dead. She saw killing Skywalker as a means to get enough money to buy her own [[Light freighter/Legends|light freighter]] and become a [[Free-trader]], working only for herself. Cheran decided to take the bribe. [[Guri]] paid her through a net of dummy corporations, which would be traced to [[Saber Enterprises]].

At the Rogues' [[Rebel base (Kile)|temporary base]] on [[Kile]], she tampered with the [[Unidentified R2 unit (Wes Janson)|R2 unit]] of [[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson's]] [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing fighter]] so that it would take control and fire on Skywalker's. As the battle concluded, Janson's R2 took control of the X-wing and started to hunt and shoot Luke's. However Luke managed to get behind him and shoot a precise hit to his R2.

Investigating on the aftermath, Luke and [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge]] found that Janson's droid had been programmed by their technician. When they went to interrogate her, she attempted to shoot Luke, before being shot by Wedge.

After that incident, they investigated her account and tracked down the bribe to Saber Enterprises. Xizor's plan was foiled, however as he intended, everyone believed that Vader was behind the attempt.
